name: Lea Martinez
ai_summary: Lea Martinez is an elementary education student from Augusta, GA with extensive childcare experience through six siblings and babysitting. She seeks part-time availability (weekends plus Mon/Wed/Fri with full Tue/Thu) and resolves conflicts by separating children for one-on-one positive discussions.

transcripts.q3: Hello. My name is Leo Martinez and I have six siblings going through the ages of 13 to 21 years old. I've babysit my neighbors children and done such my the reason I love being with children and teaching children, things is just for the greater good. I love being there for kids, watching kids, and all in all honesty. I really just like Like taking care of kids, it's just a passion of mine and that's why I'm currently going to because University and majoring in The Bachelors of Science in elementary education.
transcripts.q4: I live in Augusta, Georgia, and I'm willing to travel up to 30 minutes for shift.
transcripts.q5: I'm more interested on the part-time version of this with my availability. I'm pretty much available on weekends and on Mondays. Wednesdays and Fridays. I'm fully available on Tuesdays and Thursdays. I'm available in early mornings before 11:00 and then after 3 p.m. I'm available, but I'm more of like I'm like fully available Mondays Wednesdays and Fridays.
transcripts.q6: If in the classroom one, child hits, another child. How would deal with that is honestly based on my personal experiences with my siblings sometimes children can get upset and not know how to express those emotions besides hitting and obviously, that's not, okay. But the best way to handle that in my opinion is have a talk with both kids separately. It's best to not have them in the same area even After kind of like, well, we're talking is best to talk about them one-on-one and different groups. Different parts of the room. So they're not just saying like, oh well, I said this and they're lying or yeah. It's just good to understand both sides and a hopefully overcome that with like kind of positive talking and saying, hey like because sometimes children realize they can be in the wrong and sometimes you just need a moment, they need a moment to express those feelings to you. In a good way for the situation to like, be okay. Is to be like, hey like I'm glad you. So we sorted this out, we can give each other a fist bump or we can just say like I'm sorry or just like a foot pump. Something that's fun and will lighten up the mood for the kids.
transcripts.q7: I feel like I would make a great up kid teacher, because of the schedule schedule being so flexible, it'll give me time to expand my learning with children, as well as being able to be fully involved. I feel like the schedule scheduling will work really well with me. And honestly, I feel like with having as many siblings as I do, it'll be a very easy transition into moving with childcare both. My parents were teachers at a point and even like when I was older, my mom would bring me to the classroom if we had like certain things like after school and it was always the funnest just kind of being there and watching her teach and it's it would be like a dream of mine to do that thing.
